one two ~~~three~~~

No <textarea> here either.

This is okay though.

        as well as code blocks

    or, naturally, simple paragraphs.

[^unused]: This is unused.

markdown:
> [!NOTE]
> Useful information that users should know, even when skimming content.

Note

Useful information that users should know, even when skimming content.
note: GitHub renders [!TYPE] blockquotes as a styled <div>. Structure/classes are stable; the inline octicon SVG path is omitted here because it is volatile. Not part of the formal GFM spec.

markdown: This sentence uses `$` delimiters to show math inline: $\sqrt{3x-1}+(1+x)^2$
html: This sentence uses $ delimiters to show math inline: \sqrt{3x-1}+(1+x)^2
note: Inline $...$ math. GitHub preserves the LaTeX and renders it client-side with MathJax; the exact server-emitted wrapper is undocumented/volatile, so the html shown is illustrative of the contract (expression preserved), not byte-exact.

The ```math fenced block is a GitHub renderer feature: instead of producing a <pre><code> block, the content is rendered as display math by MathJax. html is illustrative, not byte-exact; not in the formal spec.

A ```mermaid fenced block is handed to mermaid.js and rendered as an SVG diagram in the browser. The exact server-side container markup varies; html shown is illustrative. Not part of the formal GFM spec.
GitHub replaces :name: gemoji shortcodes with Unicode characters (older output wrapped them in ; custom repo emoji become ). Output is gemoji-version dependent. Not part of the formal GFM spec.
